attackScenario: Docker containers with -v /tmp:/tmp shared volume-mount and identical effective UID (e.g., node user UID 1000) share the state dir at /tmp/speckit-claude-hooks/<project-hash>/ because getProjectHash()=sha256(cwd).slice(0,12) is UID-independent. Same-cwd containers collide, enabling cross-container session leakage under same-UID + same-cwd + shared-tmpfs.
prerequisites: Docker deployment with explicit -v /tmp:/tmp volume mount
escalatedTo: P1-worsened (blast radius only, not class of attack)

attackScenario: context.ts silent catch on ensureCodeGraphReady() masks auto-index failure; combined with missing canonicalReadiness/trustState vocabulary (R6-P1-001), callers that key off status:'ok' without inspecting readiness.freshness cannot distinguish 'empty graph, no scan ran' from 'scan auto-trigger silently failed'.
prerequisites: Auto-index failure (disk full, sqlite locked, tree-sitter-wasm load failure)
Code-graph consumer that trusts status:'ok' without inspecting readiness.freshness
escalatedTo: P1 (no change — confirmation of parity gap, not new P0)

attackScenario: None of the 5 expanded-scope threat classes (multi-UID POSIX, Docker shared-tmp, non-POSIX, MCP args injection, supply-chain postinstall, prototype-pollution, sibling cache poisoning) promotes an existing P1 finding to P0. Review's POSIX 0o600/0o700 + same-UID severity anchoring holds under adversarial expansion.

resolution: No P0 escalation confirmed under expanded scope. One P1-worsened (R2-P1-001 under Docker shared-tmp, blast-radius only, operator-misconfig-gated). One P1 confirmed (R6-P1-001 sibling asymmetry is parity-only, no cache-poisoning because no caller consumes both context.ts and query.ts in a single decision path). Supply-chain (single benign postinstall), MCP args injection (memory-ingest traversal checks + allow-list, no other fs/shell sinks), and prototype-pollution (Zod strips unknown keys, no spread-into-target spread) surfaces are clean.

direction: Multi-UID POSIX cross-UID read on macOS
reason: os.tmpdir() returns per-user /var/folders/*/T on macOS; ruled out by platform design
Atypical deployment; if configured, user has accepted shared-filesystem trust model. Not a framework flaw
30+ parse sites sampled; all cast to Record<string, unknown> and read via explicit field access or Zod-parsed (strips unknown keys). No Object.assign/spread-into-target gadget found
Only one postinstall (record-node-version.js) writes local marker file. No network, no shell spawn. mcp_server package.json has zero lifecycle scripts
memory-ingest.ts uses hasTraversalSegment() + fs.realpathSync() + ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS allow-list. No other handler takes args.filePath or args.specFolder directly into fs/shell sinks
No caller in mcp_server/ consumes BOTH handleCodeGraphContext and handleCodeGraphQuery in a single decision path. Cache poisoning requires a comparison point that doesn't exist

direction: Zod prototype pollution at MCP boundary
reason: Zod strips unknown keys by default; HookStateSchema.safeParse() drops __proto__ keys
